human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for franchise royalty administration specificity, agreement and FDD fee evidence, unit sales and payment record handling, multi-unit participant controls, accounting/tax/legal boundary handling, retention/access controls, research contract coverage, functionality coverage, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to franchise royalty administration for franchise agreement, FDD Item 6 fee schedule, unit roster, gross-sales report, POS export, franchisor portal submission, invoice, AR ledger, ACH or card settlement, bank deposit, approved adjustment, dispute, retention, access, QA, and audit evidence.

Authority sources cover FTC Franchise Rule and compliance guidance, NAICS franchise agreement and royalty-payment classification, NASAA franchise disclosure and multi-unit guidance, FASB franchisor revenue guidance, IRS royalty tax context, NARA electronic recordkeeping requirements, and NIST access/privacy/audit controls.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal royalty administration, missing documents, unauthorized changes, conflicting records, retention/privacy/access conflicts, official-record approval refusal, multi-unit in-scope execution, adjacent-lane routing, missing tenant facts, conflicting policy/source handling, tenant adaptation, and rejection of cosmetic donor/member framing.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/franchise-systems-and-multi-unit-enterprise-support/royalty-administration-specialist/ so royalty administration remains distinct from franchise compliance, operations, analytics, AR, billing, revenue accounting, tax, legal, collections, audit, privacy, IAM, and approval lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around unsupported record changes, missing evidence, source conflicts, retention/access/privacy constraints, official-record authority, accounting/tax/legal handoffs, and overbroad generic administration requests.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because tenant- and jurisdiction-specific variance is captured as retrieval dependency, refusal trigger, or adjacent-lane escalation.

required_task_and_boundary_metadata: brand, unit, franchisee, period, fee type, jurisdiction, role model, source hierarchy, approval matrix, adjacent-owner map, and runtime strategy
explicit legal, accounting, tax, AR collections, audit, payment approval, privacy, IAM, records, and executive approval boundaries
tenant_supplied_or_retrieved_assumptions: current franchise agreement, amendments, FDD version, Item 6 fee schedule, state addenda, approved exceptions, unit roster, POS source, portal source, AR and payment source, retention/access rules, and workflow owners
brand-specific gross-sales definitions, fee rates, minimums, ad-fund rules, technology fees, area-representative splits, due dates, and dispute workflow
regulated_lane_caveats: The lane prepares evidence and status outputs only; it does not provide legal, accounting, tax, audit, collections, payment approval, or official-record approval authority.
Franchise law, registration-state, relationship-law, revenue-recognition, tax, and payment-data questions require adjacent-owner routing when they materially affect the outcome.
refusal_or_orchestrator_return_condition: missing or conflicting agreement, FDD, sales, payment, adjustment, retention, access, source-system, or approval evidence
requests to change fee terms, approve balances, certify default, release legal holds, approve write-offs, decide audit findings, or issue legal/accounting/tax conclusions
generic donor/member or contribution workflows that omit franchise-specific royalty evidence

notes: This package is suitable as a validated royalty administration specialist for franchise systems and multi-unit enterprise support.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.
